title: eriogonum zapatoense
intro: Eriogonum zapatoense, also known as Zapato Buckwheat, is a rare species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family, characterized by its compact growth habit and small white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eriogonum zapatoense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea or tincture, with some cultures also using it to make a medicinal oil or salve for topical application.
